column1: C[alasdair.clarke: How are you? You missed a really great weekend up on Skye. Managed to win 4/6 games :)

Ethelor: Ive been playing a little bit on IGS though, strong players there

alasdair.clarke: Good. I need to find time to try and find some go players. but am enjoying my noew job, new department etc.
alasdair.clarke: Hope you can come viist for a game sometime soonish

C[alasdair.clarke: That was fun... board was symmetric before you approached, so I programmed in two different joseki choices depending on which way you went. Would of been a one space low pincer if you had played O3. Just cause :p

C[alasdair.clarke: Well, exactly, so maybe playing in that area was wrong full stop
C[Ethelor: Ofcourse not, because if you dont then I'll get 100% of the points or you'll have to invade later and have even less prospects than you had just then.
Ethelor: So the only time I would leave it is if the points you make by playing elsewhre is larger than me playing there
C[Ethelor: It is overconcentrated, you are correct there. But seeing that it was my potential to start with that's a very good result for black
C[alasdair.clarke: I could of continued to build my framework isntead though
Ethelor: Sure you could have but keep in mind how open that framework is :/ Bigger isn't better when it comes to framework it really depends on your skill to hold onto your potential
Ethelor: Which you may or may not have
Ethelor: So its up to you if you like to play that was I suppose :)
Ethelor: but thats a stylistic choice
Ethelor: Not better or worse, just different. Such is go
